seo.title: 10 Best Hotels in Chiang Khan 2026 — Budget to Boutique
seo.metaDescription: Find the best hotel in Chiang Khan for your budget. Our 2026 guide covers 10 top-rated properties from budget guesthouses to riverside boutique stays — with real prices, locations and honest tips.

intro: Chiang Khan's accommodation scene reflects the town's slow-life character — no towering chain hotels here, just intimate guesthouses, converted wooden shophouses and riverside boutique stays. Most properties sit along or near the Walking Street (Soi 1-2), putting you within steps of the night market, morning alms-giving route and Mekong River views. Prices are excellent value by Thai standards; even the most charming boutique rooms rarely exceed 3,500 THB per night. Below are 10 properties across budget, mid-range and boutique categories.

hotels.name: Chiang Khan River House
hotels.description: A friendly, no-frills guesthouse in a traditional wooden shophouse right on the Walking Street. Rooms are basic but clean, with fans or air-conditioning, and the shared balcony overlooking the Mekong is the real draw. The owner speaks English and can arrange Phu Thok transport. Perfect for backpackers and solo travellers.
highlights: Direct Walking Street location with Mekong views from shared balcony
Fan rooms from 350 THB; A/C rooms from 500 THB
Friendly English-speaking owner with local knowledge
Bicycle rental available on-site
Steps from the morning alms-giving route

hotels.name: Loei Village Hostel
hotels.description: A modern hostel offering both dorm beds and private rooms in a clean, well-maintained building a short walk from the Walking Street. Common areas include a garden lounge and a communal kitchen. The hostel attracts a mix of Thai and international backpackers and runs regular social events.
highlights: Dorm beds from 250 THB — cheapest option in town
Private rooms available from 600 THB
Communal kitchen and garden lounge
5-minute walk to Walking Street and Mekong riverside
Free WiFi and luggage storage

hotels.name: Khiang Hom Homestay
hotels.description: A charming family-run homestay in a restored wooden house on Soi 2. Rooms are simple with wooden floors, ceiling fans and shared bathrooms, but the warm hospitality and home-cooked breakfast (included) make it feel like staying with a Thai family. The owners are locals who know every hidden corner of Chiang Khan.
highlights: Home-cooked Thai breakfast included in the rate
Authentic wooden shophouse with traditional Lao-Thai character
Family-run with genuine local hospitality
On Soi 2 of the Walking Street — central location
Can arrange local tours and Phu Thok sunrise transport

hotels.name: Chiang Khan Gallery Resort
hotels.description: A well-designed mid-range resort set in a garden compound along the Mekong, just a 10-minute walk east of the Walking Street. Rooms are modern and spacious with air-conditioning, private balconies and river or garden views. The on-site restaurant serves Thai food with a view, and there is a small pool. Good balance of comfort and value.
highlights: Mekong River views from private balconies on select rooms
Swimming pool — rare for Chiang Khan accommodation
On-site restaurant with Thai and Isaan cuisine
Spacious, modern rooms with reliable air-conditioning
10-minute walk or 3-minute bike ride to Walking Street

hotels.name: The One Chiang Khan
hotels.description: A modern boutique-style hotel near the Walking Street offering well-appointed rooms with contemporary Thai design, quality bedding and excellent hot showers. The rooftop terrace has Mekong views and serves coffee and drinks. The property strikes a good balance between traditional Chiang Khan charm and modern amenities.
highlights: Rooftop terrace with Mekong River views
Modern rooms with quality mattresses and linens
Walking distance to night market and alms-giving route
Helpful front desk staff with local recommendations
Bicycle rental included for guests

hotels.name: Rim Khong Resort
hotels.description: A popular riverside resort with wooden bungalows set in a garden directly on the Mekong. Each bungalow has a private veranda facing the river — perfect for watching the sunset with a cold drink. The property has an old-Thai feel with teak furnishings and river-stone pathways. The location between the Walking Street and Kaeng Khut Khu is convenient.
highlights: Wooden bungalows with private river-facing verandas
Lush garden setting directly on the Mekong bank
Traditional Thai-style teak furnishings and decor
On-site riverside restaurant serving Thai and Isaan food
Central location between Walking Street and Kaeng Khut Khu

hotels.name: Baan Rimkong
hotels.description: A beautifully restored traditional wooden shophouse on the Walking Street that has been converted into a boutique guesthouse. Rooms feature original hardwood floors, high ceilings and antique Thai furnishings blended with modern comfort. The downstairs cafe serves excellent coffee and the location directly on Soi 1 is unbeatable for alms-giving and the night market.
highlights: Restored heritage shophouse with original hardwood features
Prime Walking Street location on Soi 1
On-site cafe serving specialty coffee and Thai breakfast
Antique Thai decor blended with modern amenities
Steps from the morning alms-giving procession route

hotels.name: Chiang Khan River Mountain Resort
hotels.description: The most upscale property in Chiang Khan, set on a hillside overlooking the Mekong with panoramic river and mountain views. Spacious rooms and private villas feature floor-to-ceiling windows, rainfall showers and private balconies. The infinity pool faces the Mekong, and the restaurant serves refined Thai-Isaan cuisine. A romantic retreat for couples seeking quiet luxury in a riverside setting.
highlights: Infinity pool with panoramic Mekong River and Laotian mountain views
Spacious rooms and private villas with premium amenities
On-site restaurant serving refined Thai-Isaan cuisine
Quiet hillside setting away from the Walking Street bustle
Spa treatments available with river-view treatment rooms
Complimentary shuttle to Walking Street

hotels.name: Rai Saeng Arun
hotels.description: A highly regarded boutique resort meaning 'Farm of Morning Light', Rai Saeng Arun is built from reclaimed wood in a lush riverside garden setting. The property emphasises sustainability and local craftsmanship, with each room featuring handwoven textiles and locally made furniture. The riverside restaurant uses organic ingredients from its own garden. A peaceful, design-conscious retreat.
highlights: Built from reclaimed wood with sustainable design philosophy
Lush riverside garden with Mekong views
Farm-to-table restaurant using on-site organic garden produce
Handwoven local textiles and artisan furniture in every room
Cultural activities including weaving workshops and local village visits
Highly awarded for sustainable tourism practices

hotels.name: Serene at Chiang Khan
hotels.description: A contemporary boutique hotel with minimalist Thai design set along the Mekong riverfront. Clean lines, natural materials and floor-to-ceiling glass create bright, airy rooms with river views. The on-site cafe and lounge overlook the water, and the property's quiet gardens provide a serene counterpoint to the lively Walking Street just 5 minutes away on foot.
highlights: Contemporary minimalist Thai design with natural materials
River-facing rooms with floor-to-ceiling windows
On-site lounge and cafe overlooking the Mekong
Quiet riverside garden setting
5-minute walk to Walking Street and night market

bookingTips: Book well in advance for Thai long weekends (especially Loy Krathong in November and New Year) — Chiang Khan's best properties sell out weeks ahead and prices can double.
Weekday stays are significantly quieter and often 20–30% cheaper than Friday–Saturday rates.
Request a Mekong-facing room when booking — the sunrise and sunset views from your balcony are worth any small price premium.

Most Chiang Khan accommodation is small (5–20 rooms) and independently run. Book through Booking.com or Agoda for the best selection; many smaller guesthouses are not listed on international platforms.
Air-conditioning matters in March–May (hot season) but is unnecessary November–January when mornings are genuinely cool. Fan rooms are 30–40% cheaper.
Several guesthouses include Thai breakfast in the room rate — ask when booking, as this can represent significant savings at budget level.
Use a Wise or Revolut card to pay at hotels — always pay in THB to get the real exchange rate.
